slides.title: Your automation didn't *fail*.
slides.body: Your team never agreed on what "done" meant.

slides.title: Ask three engineers
slides.body: When is a switch provisioning job actually "done"?

slides.title: Now automate it
slides.body:
The script picks *one* definition —
and ships the other two as incomplete.

slides.title: Runs green every time
slides.body:
The automation worked perfectly.
The outcome was still *wrong*.

slides.title: The part no runbook covers
slides.body: Automation encodes a *definition of done*. It won't resolve what the team never agreed on.

slides.title: Automation only executes your agreement
slides.body:
It doesn't create alignment.
Where does your "done" disagree?

Follow *@mzsnetworks*
